name: Priority Hints
description: Priority Hints (fetchpriority attribute) allow developers to signal the relative priority of resources to the browser. By setting fetchpriority=high or fetchpriority=low on images, scripts, and links, developers can optimize Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) and improve perceived loading performance.
advantages: Fine-grained control over resource loading priority
Direct impact on Core Web Vitals, especially LCP
Works with images, iframes, scripts, and links
Can significantly improve perceived page load speed
disadvantages: Relatively new API with partial browser support
Browser may override hints based on its own heuristics
Requires understanding of browser loading pipeline
Can worsen performance if used incorrectly
